LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS FREDERIC STOECKEL & SOLAZYME, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST MR. STOECKEL. PLAINTIFFS HAVE LEAVE TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THAT MR. STOECKEL PARTICIPATED IN, DIRECTED, OR AUTHORIZED THE WRONGDOING. THE COURT OVERRULES SOLAZYME'S DEMURER TO THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE PLAINTIFF SEEKS PROSPECTIVE RELIEF. THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRERS TO THE 5TH, 6TH, AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD THESE FRAUD CLAIMS WITH SPECIFICITY. THE DEMURRER TO THE CONVERSION CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THE COURT SUSTAINS WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE DEMURRER TO THE UCL CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD THE UNLAWFUL OR FRAUDULENT CONDUCT WITH MORE PARTICULARITY. THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANT'S OTHER ARGUMENTS ON THE UCL CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT RECOGNIZES THAT THERE IS NO DISTINCT CAUSE OF ACTION FOR AN INJUNCTION, UNJUST ENRICHMENT, OR CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST. UNDER CCP 452, THE COURT VIEWS THE ALLEGATIONS AS GENERAL CHARGING ALLEGATIONS OR A REMEDY REQUEST AND OVERRULES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Sep-05-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT SEPHORA S.A's motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction - The motion to quash is denied. Plaintiff establishes specific jurisdiction and defendant fails to present evidence showing that the exercise of jurisdiction would be unreasonable. Plaintiff shows that "the defendant has purposefully availed himself or herself of forum benefits; (2) the controversy is related to or arises out of the defendant's contacts with the forum; and (3) the assertion of personal jurisdiction would comport with fair play and substantial justice." Pavlovich v. Sup. Crt. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 262, 269. Defendant and Solazyme agreed that defendant would distribute the California products in Europe and the Middle East for 2 years. Defendant availed himself of a California benefit by obtaining access to the California products. The controversy arises from defendant's California contacts because plaintiff allegedly had an exclusive agreement with Solazyme and defendant's conduct allegedly interfered with that agreement. It is undisputed that part of the defendant's contract was to be performed in CA. It is proper for the court to exercise jurisdiction in this case because defendant had continuous contacts with California, purposefully derived a benefit from those activities, and it was fair to require defendant to account in California for the consequences that arose from such activities. Lastly, defendant fails to present any evidence showing how the exercise of jurisdiction is unreasonable. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Teanna L. Ward, CSR #11918

Off Calendar Feb-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SEPHORA USA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE to the declaratory relief cause of action because plaintiff seeks prospective relief. The Court sustains with 15 days leave to amend the demurrer to the UCL cause of action. Plaintiff has leave to plead the unlawful or fraudulent conduct with more particularity. The Court rejects defendant's other arguments on the UCL cause of action. The Court overrules as to the two interference causes of action. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges knowledge and wrongful conduct that is distinct from the interference itself. The Court recognizes that there is no distinct cause of action for an injunction, unjust enrichment, or constructive trust. Under CCP 452, the Court views the allegations as general charging allegations or a remedy request and overrules. The Court overrules as to the accounting cause of action. Order signed in open Court.
